story.iWant: time-based partitioning and BRIN indexes on the event store
story.soThat: query performance remains acceptable as event volume grows

tasks.task: Measure query performance: aggregate event retrieval
Commit: "Add event store partitioning and BRIN indexes"

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: Flyway migration V002__partitioning_setup.sql implements monthly partitioning on domain_event_entry
Flyway migration V003__brin_indexes.sql creates BRIN indexes on timestamp and aggregate_identifier
Partition creation script for automatic monthly partition generation
Performance test validates query performance with 100K+ events
Integration test validates events are correctly partitioned by timestamp
Query performance meets <200ms target for aggregate event retrieval

doc.title: Product Requirements Document
doc.snippet: The system shall provide PostgreSQL-based event store with partitioning, BRIN indexes, snapshots for performance at 100K+ events scale. API p95 latency <200ms, event processing lag <10s.

doc.title: Decision Architecture
doc.snippet: Monthly time-based partitioning on domain_event_entry reduces query time from O(n) to O(partition_size). Partition by RANGE on timestamp column. BRIN indexes optimized for time-range queries (timestamp-based event retrieval). HikariCP connection pooling with optimal settings to minimize latency.

doc.title: Epic 2 Technical Specification
doc.snippet: Monthly time-based partitioning on domain_event_entry. BRIN indexes on timestamp and aggregate_identifier. Performance target: <200ms for aggregate event retrieval with 100K+ events. Partition creation script for automatic monthly partition generation.

doc.title: Test Strategy
doc.snippet: Load Test Scenarios (Story 2.13): 100 concurrent users, 1000 requests/second sustained, 10K+ widgets in projection. Performance regression tests in nightly CI/CD. Baseline measurements documented for regression detection.

module.name: framework/persistence
module.purpose: Event Store partitioning and BRIN index optimizations

file.snippet: CREATE TABLE domain_event_entry (global_index BIGSERIAL PRIMARY KEY, aggregate_identifier VARCHAR(255), sequence_number BIGINT, timestamp VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL)

dependency.name: postgresql
dependency.usage: PostgreSQL JDBC driver for partitioning features

dependency.name: flyway-database-postgresql
dependency.usage: Flyway PostgreSQL dialect for partition DDL

dependency.name: testcontainers-postgresql
dependency.usage: Real PostgreSQL for integration and performance tests

constraints.constraint: NO wildcard imports - Every import must be explicit
Flyway migrations immutable - Never modify V002/V003 after deployment
Use PostgreSQL 16.10 partitioning features - PARTITION BY RANGE
Performance target: <200ms for aggregate event retrieval (100K+ events)
BRIN indexes only (not B-tree) - optimized for sequential time-series inserts
Monthly partitioning strategy - partition key is timestamp column
Partition maintenance script must be idempotent (CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS)
ANALYZE table after index creation for accurate statistics

pattern.name: PostgreSQL Monthly Partitioning Pattern
BRIN Index Pattern
pattern.code:
-- BRIN (Block Range Index) for time-series data -- Much smaller than B-tree, ideal for sequential inserts CREATE INDEX idx_domain_event_timestamp ON domain_event_entry USING BRIN (time_stamp);
ANALYZE domain_event_entry; -- Update statistics

pattern.name: Partition Maintenance Script Pattern

standards.containers: Testcontainers PostgreSQL for real partitioning behavior

ideas.idea: Test V002 migration creates partitioned table (ALTER TABLE ... PARTITION BY RANGE)
Test V002 creates initial 4 monthly partitions (current + next 3 months)
Verify partition boundaries: FROM inclusive, TO exclusive
Test V003 migration creates BRIN index on timestamp column
Test V003 creates BRIN index on aggregate_identifier column
Verify index type is BRIN (not B-tree): SELECT indexname, indexdef FROM pg_indexes
Test partition creation script is idempotent (CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS)
Test script creates partition for next month
Test script handles date arithmetic correctly
Performance test: insert 100K events across multiple aggregates
Performance test: measure aggregate event retrieval time (SELECT WHERE aggregate_identifier)
Performance test: verify query uses partition pruning (EXPLAIN output)
Document BRIN index benefits (smaller size, sequential insert performance)
Integration test: insert events with different timestamps
Integration test: verify events distributed across correct partitions (pg_stat_user_tables)
Performance test: assert query time <200ms for 100K events
Performance test: compare partitioned vs non-partitioned performance (baseline)
